understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move one or more selectable pieces (represented by 'P' and 'S') such that a pushable block (represented by 'B') is placed onto the goal tile(s) (represented by '+') in the semantic grid. The level is won when the objective(s) defined by the engine (typically block on goal) are satisfied.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based, with each action moving the currently selected piece by one cell in the chosen direction if not blocked by a wall (#) or another object. Pushing occurred when a pushable block was adjacent in the movement direction and could be pushed into an open space. Walls acted as impassable barriers that blocked movement and pushing. There were multiple selectable pieces (P and S); switching between them was done via the CLICK action with precise grid coordinates, allowing for coordinated movement and solving multi-piece puzzles.
